summary: Cached Proxy handler objects as module-level singletons in node.js and container.js. getProxyProcessor() now returns a lazily-initialized singleton handler per class instead of creating a new object each call. All tests pass and fingerprints correct, but no measurable improvement because toProxy() already caches the Proxy itself in proxyCache -- the handler is only allocated once per node lifetime anyway. Median of 5 runs: 142.47. Benchmark noise (138-155) exceeds any potential savings.

Inlined fromOffset and added sequential position tracking in parser. getPosition() now caches lineToIndex array on parser instance, tracks lastLine from previous call, and uses a fast O(1) path that checks same-line or next-5-lines before falling back to binary search. Eliminates intermediate {col,line} object allocation. METRIC_A: 55.07 (was 58.67), METRIC_B: 364.11 (was 384.77). Median across 8 runs ~125ms. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Pre-computed static event arrays for rule/comment/document/root node types. Cached keyed event arrays for decl (by prop) and atrule (by name) using null-prototype lookup table. Inlined walkSync dispatch with type-specific branches and pre-computed direct listener references to eliminate hash lookups in the hot loop. Median of 10 runs: 131.12 (4.5ms improvement). METRIC_A: ~55-72 (no regression), METRIC_B: ~313-387 (improved). All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.

Cached visitor template on Processor object to skip repeated for-in enumeration and regex validation in prepareVisitors(). Added fast path for static-only plugins (no prepare()) and template-based handling for prepare() plugins. Pre-allocated constant event arrays. System under heavy load (all metrics ~70% inflated). Even under normal load, savings estimated <1ms because the benchmark plugins use prepare(), making the static cache empty. The real bottleneck is parsing/stringifying/walking, not prepareVisitors() itself. All 649 tests pass, fingerprints correct.
